intro.uz: Iqtisodchi E.F. Schumacher "Small is Beautiful" kitobida ochkoʻzlik va hasadga qurilgan katta iqtisod sayyorani buzayotganini, yechim esa kichik, insonparvar, barqaror iqtisodda ekanini aytadi. Til oʻrganishda ham shu qoida: har kuni ozgina koʻp narsani beradi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasi aynan kichik miqdor tili: kichik miqdorlar (a few/a little va few/little; hardly any; only/just) va daraja ravishlari — yumshatish (quite / fairly / rather / a bit).
intro.en: In "Small is Beautiful", economist E.F. Schumacher argues that a huge economy built on greed and envy is destroying the planet, and that the answer is smaller, more human, sustainable economies. Language learning follows the same rule: a little every day beats a lot rarely. This week's two grammar topics are the language of small amounts: small quantities (a few/a little vs few/little; hardly any; only/just) and degree adverbs for softening (quite / fairly / rather / a bit).

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida "a few" va "few" (yoki "a little" va "little") oʻrtasidagi ijobiy/salbiy farqni toʻgʻri ishlata olasiz va biror narsani "very" demasdan, meʼyorli ravishlar (quite / fairly / a bit) bilan yumshatib tasvirlab bera olasiz.
canDo.en: By the end you can use the positive/negative difference between "a few" and "few" (or "a little" and "little") correctly, and describe something in a moderate, softened way using quite / fairly / a bit instead of always saying "very".

grammar.titleUz: Kichik miqdorlar: a few/a little va few/little; hardly any; only/just
grammar.titleEn: Small quantities: a few/a little vs few/little
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 1 — talk about how much time, money or free time you have accurately
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can show the difference between "some" (a few/a little) and "almost none" (few/little)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida "biroz/ozgina" (ijobiy) va "deyarli yoʻq/juda kam" (salbiy) alohida soʻzlar, shuning uchun oʻzbek oʻquvchi "a little" va "little" ni bir xil "ozgina" deb tarjima qilib yuboradi. Ingliz tilida esa oldida "a" bor-yoʻqligi maʼnoni agʻdaradi: "a little time" = biroz vaqt bor (yaxshi), "little time" = vaqt deyarli yoʻq (yomon). Yana oʻzbekchada sanaladigan/sanalmaydigan farqi yoʻq: "a few" + sanaladigan (a few minutes), "a little" + sanalmaydigan (a little money).
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I have a little friends. I have few time, please wait. ✅ I have a few friends. I have little time, so let's be quick. "a few" sanaladigan koʻplik bilan (friends), "a little" sanalmaydigan bilan (time); "a" ni tashlab qoʻysangiz maʼno "deyarli yoʻq"ga aylanadi.

examples.en: Study just a little English every day.
examples.uz: Har kuni atigi bir oz ingliz tili oʻqing.

examples.en: I have a few good friends, and that is enough.
examples.uz: Bir nechta yaqin doʻstim bor, shuning oʻzi yetarli.

examples.en: There were hardly any people in the shop.
examples.uz: Doʻkonda deyarli hech kim yoʻq edi.

exercises.prompt: I only have ___ minutes, so let's be quick.
exercises.hintUz: minutes — sanaladigan koʻplik, ijobiy "biroz" → a few

exercises.prompt: Sorry, I have very ___ time today — I'm too busy.
This dress costs ___ ten dollars — that's cheap!

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): kuniga qancha ingliz tili oʻrganasiz — "I study just a little English …" va "I know a few useful words like … ."
exercises.hintUz: a little + sanalmaydigan (English/time); a few + sanaladigan (words/friends)

grammar.titleUz: Daraja ravishlari (yumshatish): quite / fairly / rather / a bit
grammar.titleEn: Degree adverbs for softening: quite / fairly / a bit
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Fluency — hedge and moderate your opinions instead of always saying "very"
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2: I can soften and grade an adjective (quite/fairly/a bit) rather than overusing "very"
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek oʻquvchi hamma narsaga "very" qoʻyadi (very good, very difficult) — bu A2 ohang. B2 nutqi meʼyorlaydi: ijobiy, muloyim maqtov uchun quite/fairly (ancha yaxshi, yetarlicha yaxshi), kutilmagan yoki noxush narsa uchun rather (ancha qiyin), salbiy sifatni yumshatish uchun a bit (biroz qimmat). Oʻzbekchada "ancha, xiyla, biroz" bor, ammo ular ingliz tilidagidek qatʼiy darajaga bogʻlanmagan.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ The book is very very difficult. It's a bit beautiful. ✅ The book is fairly difficult. It's quite beautiful. Kuchaytirish uchun "very"ni takrorlamang — pastroq daraja uchun fairly/quite ishlating; "a bit" faqat noxush sifat bilan keladi (a bit expensive), ijobiy sifat bilan emas.

examples.en: The book is fairly difficult, but I recommend it.
examples.uz: Kitob yetarlicha qiyin, lekin tavsiya qilaman.

examples.en: These ideas are quite common now.
examples.uz: Bu gʻoyalar hozir ancha keng tarqalgan.

examples.en: That dress is a bit pricey for me.
examples.uz: Bu koʻylak men uchun biroz qimmatroq.

exercises.prompt: The level of English in this book is ___ difficult.
exercises.hintUz: sifat oldida meʼyorli daraja → fairly difficult (yetarlicha qiyin)

exercises.prompt: Hmm, that's ___ expensive. Can you give me a better price?
exercises.hintUz: noxush sifatni yumshatish → a bit expensive (biroz qimmatroq)

exercises.prompt: Her English is ___ good — better than she thinks.
exercises.hintUz: ijobiy, muloyim maqtov → quite good (ancha yaxshi)

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting: bugungi kuningizni "very" ishlatmasdan tasvirlang — "Today I'm a bit … , the weather is quite … , my work is fairly … ."
exercises.hintUz: a bit + noxush sifat; quite/fairly + ijobiy sifat; "very"ni ishlatmang

vocab.example: Our cultures cultivate greed and envy.

vocab.defEn: jealousy; a bad feeling when someone else has something good
wild, uncontrolled activity done without control
vocab.example: Michael got into a frenzy about Jeff's hair.

vocab.uz: isteʼmolchilik (koʻproq sotib olishni hayotning asosiy maqsadi deb bilish)
vocab.defEn: the belief that buying things is the most important thing in life
vocab.example: Modern culture is driven by consumerism.

vocab.defEn: to slow down, lessen or stop
vocab.example: His preoccupation did not abate; it got stronger.

vocab.defEn: being very cruel and tough, showing no mercy
vocab.example: The richest societies pursue money with great ruthlessness.

vocab.uz: insoniylashtirmoq, insonparvar qilmoq, odamga qulay qilmoq
vocab.defEn: to make something kinder and better for people
vocab.example: We should humanize work so it serves people.

vocab.uz: bir xil, yakrang, zerikarli (bir ishni qayta-qayta qilish)
vocab.defEn: boring because it is always the same
vocab.example: A factory job can be very monotonous.
This is absurd. Just buy a wig.

vocab.uz: xayolni butunlay band qilgan narsa; oʻta berilganlik
vocab.defEn: something you think about all the time; almost an obsession
vocab.example: We have a preoccupation with money.

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda xaridor ikki koʻylak uchun savdolashadi va oxir-oqibat arzon narxga kelishadi. Bu darsning gʻoyasiga mos: Schumacher isteʼmolchilik — doim koʻproq, koʻproq sotib olish istagi — hech qachon susaymaydi (does not abate) deb ogohlantiradi. Sizningcha, xaridorga ikkala koʻylak ham chindan kerak edimi, yoki u shunchaki yaxshi savdodan zavq oldimi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue a shopper bargains over two dresses and finally gets a cheap price. It fits the lesson theme: Schumacher warns that consumerism — always wanting to buy more and more — never abates. Do you think the buyer really needed both dresses, or just enjoyed a good deal?

keyVocab.defEn: a good deal; something bought at a cheap price
to look in many stores to find the best price
a lowering of the price of something
too expensive; more than I can afford
a chance to buy something at a low price

quiz.q: How far is it around the world measured at the equator (the circumference)?
quiz.qUz: Yer sharining ekvator boʻylab aylanasi (uzunligi) taxminan qancha?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: b) 40,000 km. Rob Yerning aylanasi 40,075 km ekanini aytadi. Neil c) 50,000 km dedi va xato qildi. Mavzuga mos: butun dunyoni aylanish emas, uy yonidagi kichik sarguzasht ham (microadventure) zavq beradi — kichik goʻzaldir!

vocab.uz: sarguzasht ruhi (yangi, hayajonli ishni qilish hissi)
vocab.defEn: the feeling of doing a new, exciting and sometimes dangerous activity

vocab.uz: yangi soʻz yoki ibora oʻylab topmoq
vocab.defEn: to use a word or phrase that no one has used before
an idea you have based on feelings but with no proof
part of the pleasure or enjoyment of something
very close to where you live

funEnglish.title: Forget big change, start with a tiny habit: BJ Fogg at TEDxFremont

speakingPrompt.en: Think about the idea 'small is beautiful'. What is one thing you have only a little of, but that makes you happy? Do you own too many things — and is your life quite busy or fairly simple?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: kichik miqdorlar (a little / a few; only / just; hardly any) va daraja ravishlari (quite / fairly / a bit — "very"ni ishlatmasdan).
